comment.body:
SPX 0DTE Asymmetrical Butterfly Calculation
You have an asymmetrical put butterfly spread with strikes at 5995/6005/6015 for SPX expiring today (Feb 7, 2025). Let’s break down the cost basis, breakeven points, and risk profile.
1. Position Breakdown
+6 5995 Puts @ 2.90
-9 6005 Puts @ 4.5133
+3 6015 Puts @ 7.84
2. Net Cost Calculation (Basis per spread)
Each spread consists of:
6 Long 5995 Puts @ 2.90 → Total Cost = 6 × 2.90 = 17.40
9 Short 6005 Puts @ 4.5133 → Total Credit = 9 × 4.5133 = 40.62
3 Long 6015 Puts @ 7.84 → Total Cost = 3 × 7.84 = 23.52
Net Cost = (Long 5995 Cost + Long 6015 Cost) - (Short 6005 Credit)
= (17.40 + 23.52) - 40.62
= 0.30 debit per spread
Total cash outlay (assuming each contract represents 100 SPX):
= 0.30 × 100 = $30 per spread
3. Breakeven (B/E) Points
Since this is a butterfly structure, your breakeven points are derived from the spread width and net debit:
Upper B/E: 6015 - Net Debit = 6015 - 0.30 = 6014.70
Lower B/E: 5995 + Net Debit = 5995 + 0.30 = 5995.30
4. Risk & Reward Profile
Max Risk: The debit paid ($30 per spread).
Max Profit: Achieved if SPX closes at 6005, where the short leg (6005 put) is ITM while long legs (5995 and 6015) are balanced.
Max Profit = (Width of strikes - Net Debit) × 100
= (10 - 0.30) × 100 = $970 per spread
Max Loss: Limited to the $30 per spread paid.
Profit Zone: Between 5995.30 and 6014.70.
5. Summary of Trade
Cost Basis: $30 per spread
Breakeven: 5995.30 – 6014.70
Max Profit: $970 per spread (if SPX closes at 6005)
Max Loss: $30 per spread
Risk-Reward Ratio: 1:32 (extremely favorable if SPX pins 6005)
Final Thoughts
This is a highly asymmetric 0DTE butterfly, offering a very low risk for a potentially high reward. Your best-case scenario is SPX closing right at 6005, where you'd maximize the payout.  I put a scerrn shpt of my lotto fly into cgpt  here's the ariothmatic

6 Long 5995 Puts @ 2.90 → Total Cost = 6 × 2.90 = 17.40
9 Short 6005 Puts @ 4.5133 → Total Credit = 9 × 4.5133 = 40.62
3 Long 6015 Puts @ 7.84 → Total Cost = 3 × 7.84 = 23.52
Net Cost = (Long 5995 Cost + Long 6015 Cost) - (Short 6005 Credit)
= (17.40 + 23.52) - 40.62
= 0.30 debit per spread
Total cash outlay (assuming each contract represents 100 SPX):
= 0.30 × 100 = $30 per spread
3. Breakeven (B/E) Points
Since this is a butterfly structure, your breakeven points are derived from the spread width and net debit:
Upper B/E: 6015 - Net Debit = 6015 - 0.30 = 6014.70
Lower B/E: 5995 + Net Debit = 5995 + 0.30 = 5995.30
4. Risk & Reward Profile
Max Risk: The debit paid ($30 per spread).
Max Profit: Achieved if SPX closes at 6005, where the short leg (6005 put) is ITM while long legs (5995 and 6015) are balanced.
Max Profit = (Width of strikes - Net Debit) × 100
= (10 - 0.30) × 100 = $970 per spread
Max Loss: Limited to the $30 per spread paid.
Profit Zone: Between 5995.30 and 6014.70.
5. Summary of Trade
Cost Basis: $30 per spread
Breakeven: 5995.30 – 6014.70
Max Profit: $970 per spread (if SPX closes at 6005)
Max Loss: $30 per spread
Risk-Reward Ratio: 1:32 (extremely favorable if SPX pins 6005)
Final Thoughts

Looking at the daily chart for $SPX, it looks like that is exactly what is happening- stair steps. We seem to be dropping every Friday on Trump bombs, and then climbing back up during the week, but, yes. Still lower highs as someone mentioned earlier. Just wondering if anyone else was noticing the same.

comment.body:
He had said back in January after the election gap fill on the 13th that if the market were to break new highs in the final weeks of January from that gap fill, then we’re likely to trend higher into March OPEX. 6300 is my guess 

Then if I remember correctly, if we couldn’t hold ~ 6100 into March OPEX or right after OPEX into the end of March, then we’d get a steeper, more rapid decline into 5200-5500. 

I believe this also aligns for the most part with what Alma has been saying/seeing. I think today was just noise. Everyone already knows about the tariffs. It wasn’t really “news.”

Russel 2000 broke last weeks high on Wednesday while S&P stayed in range. Russel while down, outpaced tech today. Seems risk on still, but undecided. Usually when the Russell is showing strength and holds overall trend after a flush like Monday, there’s not much downside for SPX. 

Would not shock me if we gap up Monday. Since we’ve had 2 straight weeks of tariffs on Friday, gap down Monday. Seems too easy. If we don’t gap up, maybe one last small flush on CPI just to make new highs into 2/21 like Alma has said. That’s my opinion
